tasks.title: Refactor config.js: resolveEndpoint(), setRuntimeEndpoint(), endpoint-aware apiBaseUrl/authBaseUrl
tasks.description:
Refactor src/config.js to support a runtime endpoint override and a unified resolution chain.

Implementation:
1. Add module-level variable `let _runtimeEndpoint = null;`
2. Add `setRuntimeEndpoint(ep)` function that validates 'sandbox'/'production'/null, sets _runtimeEndpoint (throw on invalid input)
3. Add `resolveEndpoint()` implementing the priority chain:
   - Return _runtimeEndpoint if non-null
   - Else if NODE_ENV==='development' → 'sandbox'
   - Else load config and return cfg?.endpoint || 'production'
4. Refactor `apiBaseUrl()`: if MAYAR_API_URL env → return it; else resolveEndpoint()==='sandbox' → 'https://api.mayar.club' else 'https://api.mayar.id'
5. Refactor `authBaseUrl()`: if MAYAR_AUTH_URL env → return it; else resolveEndpoint()==='sandbox' → 'https://auth.mayar.club' else 'https://auth.mayar.id'
6. Update `isDev()` to return resolveEndpoint() === 'sandbox' (backward compat)
7. Export `setRuntimeEndpoint` and `resolveEndpoint` alongside existing exports

Acceptance:
- setRuntimeEndpoint('sandbox') then apiBaseUrl() returns sandbox URL
- setRuntimeEndpoint(null) restores fallback to NODE_ENV / stored config
- Env vars MAYAR_API_URL/MAYAR_AUTH_URL always take precedence
- With no config and no env, defaults to production URLs
- Existing config.test.js tests pass (no breaking changes to save/load/clear)

tasks.title: Fix api.js dynamic base URL + add --sandbox/--production/--env flag parsing in cli.js
tasks.description:
Make api.js resolve the base URL dynamically on each request. Add new environment flags (--sandbox, --production, --env) to parseFlags and wire them into config.setRuntimeEndpoint() in run(). Fix the hardcoded endpoint in ensureKey().

Implementation — src/api.js:
1. Remove module-level `const BASE_URL = config.apiBaseUrl();`
2. Inside `request()`, replace `BASE_URL` with `config.apiBaseUrl()` so the URL is resolved fresh on every call

Implementation — src/cli.js parseFlags:
1. Add `--sandbox` → flags.sandbox = true
2. Add `--production` → flags.production = true
3. Add `--env <value>` → flags.env = value (the existing unknown-long-flag handler already supports --key=value form)

Implementation — src/cli.js run():
1. After parseFlags(), resolve endpoint override:
   - If both sandbox and production are truthy → error + exit(1)
   - If sandbox → setRuntimeEndpoint('sandbox')
   - Else if production → setRuntimeEndpoint('production')
   - Else if flags.env → validate 'sandbox'/'production' (error+exit otherwise), then setRuntimeEndpoint
   - Else → setRuntimeEndpoint(null)
2. In ensureKey(), replace hardcoded `endpoint: 'production'` with `endpoint: config.resolveEndpoint()` so the auto-prompt respects --sandbox
3. Update HELP global flags section: add --sandbox, --production, --env <sandbox|production>
4. Update HELP environment section: new resolution order text

Acceptance:
- `mayar --sandbox whoami` uses api.mayar.club
- `mayar --production whoami` uses api.mayar.id
- Both --sandbox and --production together errors with clear message
- Invalid --env value errors
- `--help` shows new flags and resolution order
- Existing parseFlags tests continue to pass
- ensureKey auto-prompt uses resolved endpoint, not hardcoded 'production'

tasks.title: Add interactive environment selection to mayar login
tasks.description:
Add an interactive TTY prompt to `mayar login` so users can choose between sandbox and production when no --sandbox/--production/--env flag is passed. Save the chosen endpoint to config.json alongside auth tokens.

Implementation — src/commands/login.js:
1. After ui.printBanner(), check if endpoint was selected via flags:
   - If config.resolveEndpoint() returns the default 'production' and no flags were passed AND process.stdin.isTTY:
     a. Print: "Select environment:\n  [1] Production (web.mayar.id)\n  [2] Sandbox (web.mayar.club)"
     b. Use ui.ask('Choose [1]: ') to read input
     c. Default to '1' (production) on empty input
     d. Map '1'/'production' → 'production', '2'/'sandbox' → 'sandbox'
     e. Call config.setRuntimeEndpoint(choice)
   - If NOT a TTY and no flags → proceed with production (or stored config) silently
2. Add endpoint display: `${ui.dim('Endpoint:')} ${resolved}` after the auth server line
3. After successful login, save endpoint at top level:
   - config.save({ ...existing, endpoint: config.resolveEndpoint(), auth: { ... } })
4. In --json mode: skip the interactive prompt; include endpoint in JSON output

Acceptance:
- `mayar login` (TTY, no flags) shows env prompt, defaults to production on Enter
- `mayar login --sandbox` skips prompt, uses auth.mayar.club
- After login, config.json has "endpoint" at top level matching selection
- The MayarAuth SDK is initialized with the correct auth URL
- Non-TTY proceeds with production silently

tasks.title: Add interactive environment selection to mayar init
tasks.description:
Add an interactive TTY prompt to `mayar init` so first-time users can choose between sandbox and production. Save the chosen endpoint alongside the API key in config.json.

Implementation — src/commands/init.js:
1. After ui.printBanner() and the existing API key check/overwrite logic:
   - If no --sandbox/--production/--env flag was set AND process.stdin.isTTY:
     a. Print: "Select environment:\n  [1] Production (web.mayar.id)\n  [2] Sandbox (web.mayar.club)"
     b. Use ui.ask('Choose [1]: ') to read input
     c. Default to '1' (production) on empty input
     d. Map '1'/'production' → 'production', '2'/'sandbox' → 'sandbox'
     e. Call config.setRuntimeEndpoint(choice)
   - If NOT a TTY and no flags → keep production as default
2. Update the welcome message to reference the correct web URL based on environment:
   - Replace hardcoded 'https://web.mayar.id' with the resolved web URL (web.mayar.id for production, web.mayar.club for sandbox)
3. Save endpoint: `config.save({ apiKey: key.trim(), endpoint: config.resolveEndpoint(), savedAt: ... })`
   - This replaces the hardcoded `endpoint: 'production'`
4. Add success output line: `${ui.dim('Endpoint:')} ${resolved}`

Acceptance:
- `mayar init` (TTY, no flags) shows env prompt, defaults to production
- `mayar init --sandbox` skips prompt, saves endpoint='sandbox'
- After init, config.json contains both apiKey and endpoint
- Welcome message URL matches chosen environment
- Non-TTY without flags saves production endpoint

tasks.title: Create src/commands/skill.js — fetch SKILL.md from GitHub and install to agent target directories
tasks.description:
Create a new `mayar skill install` command that fetches SKILL.md from the GitHub raw URL and writes it to multiple AI agent skill directories.

Implementation — src/commands/skill.js (new file):
1. Export `run(ctx)` where ctx = { apiKey, flags, positional }
2. Parse subcommands: first positional must be 'install'
   - No subcommand or 'help' → print usage and return
   - Unknown subcommand → error + exit(1)
3. For 'install':
   a. Determine target from flags.target (default 'all'); valid: 'all', 'agents', 'claude', 'opencode', 'codex', 'cursor'
   b. Fetch SKILL.md from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mayarid/mayar-cli/refs/heads/main/SKILL.md using https.get
      - Follow 301/302 redirects (up to 3 hops)
      - On failure: print error, exit(1)
   c. Define target files (under cwd):
      - '.agents/skills/mayar/SKILL.md' (target: agents)
      - '.claude/skills/mayar/SKILL.md' (target: claude)
      - '.opencode/skills/mayar/SKILL.md' (target: opencode)
      - '.codex/skills/mayar/SKILL.md' (target: codex)
      - '.cursor/rules/mayar.mdc' (target: cursor) — implementation in task-006
   d. For each applicable directory: create parent dirs with fs.mkdirSync({recursive:true})
      - File exists && !flags.force → print "Skipping: <path> (use --force to overwrite)"
      - Otherwise → write content, print "Installed: <path>"
   e. If --json: output JSON array of {target, path, status}

Acceptance:
- `mayar skill install` fetches SKILL.md and writes to .agents/, .claude/, .opencode/, .codex/
- `mayar skill install --target claude` only writes to .claude/
- Running twice without --force shows skip messages
- Running with --force overwrites existing files
- Invalid --target value errors
- Network errors print clear message and exit non-zero
- --json outputs machine-readable JSON
- Uses only Node.js built-in modules (https, fs, path)

tasks.title: Cursor .mdc adaptation — generate .cursor/rules/mayar.mdc with YAML frontmatter
tasks.description:
Extend the skill install command to generate a Cursor-specific .mdc rule file when --target is 'all' or 'cursor'. The .mdc file wraps SKILL.md content with Cursor-compatible YAML frontmatter.

Implementation — extend src/commands/skill.js:
1. Add function `generateCursorMdc(skillContent)` that returns the full .mdc content:
   a. Strip the SKILL.md's own YAML frontmatter (lines between first `---` and second `---`) to avoid nested delimiters
   b. Prepend Cursor .mdc frontmatter:
      ```
      ---
      alwaysApply: true
      description: Mayar CLI — interact with the Mayar payment platform (invoices, products, payments, customers, transactions, webhooks, QR codes, memberships, credit wallets, discounts, installments, bundling, SaaS/software licensing).
      globs:
        - "**/*"
      ---
      ```
   c. After frontmatter, add the remaining SKILL.md body (without original frontmatter). If the original frontmatter can't be parsed (no `---` delimiters), include raw content as-is
2. In the install loop, when target === 'cursor' (or 'all' covers cursor):
   - Generate content via generateCursorMdc()
   - Write to .cursor/rules/mayar.mdc
   - Follow same force/skip logic
   - Print "Installed (Cursor .mdc): .cursor/rules/mayar.mdc"

Acceptance:
- `mayar skill install --target cursor` creates .cursor/rules/mayar.mdc
- .mdc has valid YAML frontmatter with alwaysApply, description, globs
- No nested `---` YAML delimiters in the output
- `mayar skill install` (default all) includes Cursor .mdc
- `mayar skill install --target agents` does NOT generate .mdc
- Force/skip logic works for .mdc files too

tasks.title: Register skill command in cli.js handlers + update HELP with skill and env flags sections
tasks.description:
Wire the skill command into the CLI command routing and finalize the HELP text documentation.

Implementation — src/cli.js:
1. Add 'skill' to the `handlers` map:
   - `skill: './commands/skill'`
2. Update the HELP() function:
   a. Add a new section under Setup:
      `${ui.bold('Agent Skills:')}
        skill install [--target <all|agents|claude|opencode|codex|cursor>] [--force]`
      with description: "Install Mayar SKILL.md into AI agent directories"
   b. Verify/ensure the Global flags section includes --sandbox, --production, --env <sandbox|production> (these may have been added in task-002; check and add if missing)
   c. Verify/ensure the Environment section resolution order text is updated to the new chain (may have been added in task-002; check and add if missing)

Acceptance:
- `mayar skill install` runs the skill command
- `mayar skill` without subcommand shows skill-specific help
- `mayar -h` shows the new skill section
- Global flags section includes --sandbox, --production, --env
- Environment section shows correct resolution order
- Unknown command 'skillx' still errors
